description: Japanese language pack — A1–A2 backbone. 320 vocabulary items, 75 core phrases, 25 grammar points (with substitution ladders), 20 pronunciation rules, 27 dialogues. Content volume for vocabulary, phrases, grammar, pronunciation, and dialogues now meets the §10.1 Tier-1 targets; slangRegister (via idioms) and the lesson-based categories (listening, writing, assessment, roleplay) remain to be built.
coverage: A1 everyday basics through early A2: greetings, self-introduction, numbers/money/time, days of the week, family, body, food/drink, colors, places, transport, household objects, weather, and a broad verb/adjective/adverb base. Grammar spans the core particles (は/が, を, に/で, の), です/ます, negation, past tense, か questions, plus the A2 backbone: the て-form and its family (～ています, ～てください, ～てもいい), ～たい, potential/できる, ～から, ～が/けど, comparatives (より/のほうが), 一番, ～ましょう, ～と思います, ～でしょう, and adjective→adverb formation. Deliberately excludes slang, keigo/formality levels, and profanity — Japanese's real register axis is politeness, not crudeness, and that deserves its own design pass rather than reusing the pt-br severity model as-is.
editorialNotes: Kanji/kana/romaji were authored directly (no machine translation pass). The A1–A2 expansion (a1-extended.json, a2.json, phrases/core.json, grammar/a2.json, pronunciation/extended.json) roughly quintupled the pack's volume and should get a native speaker's sign-off before being treated as more than a draft — particle choices, transitive/intransitive pairings, naturalness of example sentences, and pitch-accent notes in particular. See NATIVE_REVIEW.md.
